evidence: Desktop capture showed navigation, media stage, action rail, and status content without requiring an entrance animation to finish.
Controls were inventoried but live interaction replay was not completed.
Desktop evidence showed white and cyan interface text against a predominantly black shell.
The responsive viewport capture was not reliable enough to verify mobile clipping or overlap.
Motion durations were observed, but reduced-motion behavior was not replayed.
The extracted system excludes logos, characters, QR codes, proprietary fonts, copy, and protected media.
Navigation, media, rails, typography, and cyan signal accents consistently follow the signal-dark profile.
The cinematic media stage and technical status rails are grounded in the game's world rather than a generic SaaS skeleton.
The first viewport is not composed from the default eyebrow, split hero, paired CTA, and three-card sequence.
Long ambient media motion supports the cinematic landing experience and remains visually subordinate to navigation.
observations.rationale: Retain the ambient motion language, subject to reduced-motion verification.

evidence: Section identity, status indicators, and actions are specific to the game and its campaign content.
Rails, counters, and navigation geometry use a consistent hard-edged visual system rather than library defaults.
The design intentionally uses near-black surfaces and a saturated cyan signal color.
observations.rationale: These choices form a coherent franchise-specific signal system and are not generic defaults.
